DISCOVERY, DEPT. 525, AS TO THE NOV-22-2024 HEARING RE: PETITIONER BRENDEN KELLEY'S HEARING ON PETITION TO ENFORCE INTERSTATE SUBPOENA AGAINST A NON-PARTY PURSUANT TO CCP 2029.600 ET SEQ; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITION IS GRANTED. HOWEVER, X CORP.'S RESPONSE TO THE SUBPOENA SHALL BE LIMITED TO THE ITEMS SET FORTH ON PAGE 1, LINES 14-16, OF X CORP.'S RESPONSE TO THE PETITION. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND EMAIL IT TO PRO TEM JUDGE ANDY SCHWARTZ. JUDGE PRO TEM: ANDY SCHWARTZ; CLERKS: M. MACADANGDANG, V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (525/JPT)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Enforce Interstate Subpoena Against A Non-Party Pursuant To Ccp 2029.600 Et Seq.
Off Calendar Nov-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing re: PETITIONER BRENDEN KELLEY's Hearing On Petition To Enforce Interstate Subpoena Against A Non-Party Pursuant To Ccp 2029.600 Et Seq.

No appearances made in this matter. The court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petition is granted. However, X Corp.'s response to the subpoena shall be limited to the items set forth on page 1, lines 14-16, of X Corp.'s response to the Petition.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and email it to Pro Tem Judge Andy Schwartz.

Judge Pro Tem: Andy Schwartz; Clerks: M. Macadangdang, V. Da Fonseca; not reported. (525/JPT)
